text  
division a reports a revenue function r_a(x) = 500x - 3x² and an expense function e_a(x) = 200x + 4x², where x is the number of units produced and sold. division b reports a revenue function r_b(y) = 400y - 2y² and an expense function e_b(y) = 150y + 5y², where y is the number of units produced and sold. if the firm is suspected of underreporting total profits by manipulating unit distributions between divisions, find the values of x and y that maximize the firm's total profit p(x, y) = (r_a(x) - e_a(x)) + (r_b(y) - e_b(y)).  
the tax accountant further discovers that the firm has been using a transfer price t = αx - βy for units transferred from division a to division b, where α and β are constants yet to be determined. given that the transfer price should ensure that the internal trading between the divisions does not lead to a tax liability exceeding 10% of the firm's total reported profit, establish a relationship between α and β. assume the firm's tax rate is 30% and use the condition that the internal tax liability is given by 0.3 · t · n ≤ 0.1 · p(x, y), where n is the number of units transferred.  
highlighted section  
the firm's total profit p(x, y) is calculated as the sum of the profits from division a and division b. the profit from division a is r_a(x) - e_a(x), and the profit from division b is r_b(y) - e_b(y). the total profit p(x, y) is maximized when the values of x and y are chosen to maximize the sum of these two profits.  
highlighted section  
the transfer price t = αx - βy is used to ensure that the internal trading between divisions does not lead to a tax liability exceeding 10% of the firm's total reported profit. the internal tax liability is calculated as 0.3 · t · n, and it must be less than or equal to 0.1 · p(x, y). the relationship between α and β is determined by ensuring that the internal tax liability does not exceed the allowable limit.
